nodes.label: Design Rule: One Row = One Experiment
Design Rule: Never Split by Contact
Augmentation Strategy: feature_noise (Gaussian noise, σ=1% std)
Augmentation Strategy: grouped_scaling (same factor per contact group)
Nested CV Compromise: tune once on hold-out train, reuse params in LOEO
Feature Extraction (13 time-domain + 3 freq-domain per segment)
safe_to_csv() with 3 retries and .new.csv fallback (Excel lock handling)

Idempotent Pipeline Steps (each step re-runnable independently)
MAE (Mean Absolute Error, µm) — Primary Metric
High p/n Ratio (~200 features vs 8 training points)
Finding: Linear Regularized Models Dominate (ElasticNet, Lasso top-10)
Finding: Tuning Worsens LOEO MAE (+0.90 µm vs baseline)
Finding: Augmentation Does Not Help (delta = +0.04 µm)
Best LOEO MAE: ~27 µm (ElasticNet, no tuning)
Statistical Significance Threshold: >5 µm difference needed with n=10
Conclusion: Need More Data, Not More Models

Limitation: Potentially Correlated Multi-contact Aggregated Features
Limitation: No Replicas (single VB_um measurement per experiment)

Design Rationale: LOEO more honest than hold-out with n=10 (R² can swing 0.9 to -2.0)
Design Rationale: GroupShuffleSplit preserves experiment-level integrity in splits
Layered Pipeline Rationale: Explicit labels enable per-cell traceability
Layered Pipeline Rationale: Random and Grid as independent branches (CT_random vs CT_grid)
